worker.notes: OpenCode GLM-5.1 produced the bounded OCR provider implementation and tests. Hermes reviewed the diff, removed unused example language config, changed endpoint-without-model behavior to fail at provider initialization instead of silently stubbing, reran validation, and committed accepted implementation.

summary: Added a real OpenAI-compatible OCR provider that sends image bytes as bounded base64 data URLs to vision chat/completions, parses plain-text or structured JSON OCR responses, supports retries/backoff, request/response byte limits, max_tokens, MIME detection, language-aware prompts, and clear error paths. Registry now creates the real OCR provider when an enabled OCR config has an endpoint and fails fast when the required model is missing; endpoint-less OCR providers continue using the stub fallback. Config loading now supports safe underscore provider env var names and TR_OCR_<NAME>_MODEL overrides without storing secrets.

tests.command: opencode run --model opencode-go/glm-5.1 --format json <bounded implementation task>
tests.notes: OpenCode used exactly opencode-go/glm-5.1 for implementation.
Initial read-only review approved after Hermes reconciliation; final read-only review also returned APPROVE with no must-fix issues.
Expo web export passed; existing expo-av deprecation warning remains.
The go binary is not installed on PATH in the Hermes supervisor environment.

blockers: Go tests remain blocked because go is not installed on PATH.
risks: OCR provider integration is available through the provider registry, but scanned PDF page rasterization/wiring into parser flow remains a future slice.
Vision OCR quality depends on the configured OpenAI-compatible provider and model.
follow_up_suggestions: Run go test ./internal/config ./internal/provider on a host with Go installed.
Add scanned-PDF rasterization/OCR pipeline wiring if scanned document support is required beyond provider availability.
Consider PDF FlateDecode or an audited PDF parser dependency for more real-world PDF coverage.
